prestazioni: Engine swap to a naturally aspirated 4.0L Rover V8 (engine code: Rover 3.5/4.0L 'Buick' V8, derived from the Triumph Stag's original 3.0L V8 but stroked to 4.0L with a forged crankshaft from a TVR Griffith 4.0, Mahle forged pistons, and a custom billet camshaft by Kent Cams (profile: 280° duration, 0.500" lift) for high-RPM power delivery (350+ hp at 6,500 RPM). Matched with a Holley Sniper EFI system (4500 series throttle body) and MSD 6AL ignition for precise fuel and spark control.

Forced induction via a Rotrex C38-91 supercharger (8.5 psi boost) with a custom intercooler setup (PWR North America bar-and-plate core, 2.5" piping) and a custom intake manifold fabricated from 6061-T6 aluminum, designed to fit the Stag's tight engine bay. Paired with a Quaife ATB differential (3.7:1 ratio) and a Tilton twin-disc clutch (7.25" diameter) for drivetrain reliability under increased torque (450+ lb-ft).

Suspension overhaul with custom-valved Öhlins Road & Track coilovers (front: 220 lb/in springs, rear: 200 lb/in springs) paired with polybushings from SuperPro (part numbers: SPF1074K for front control arms, SPF1075K for rear trailing arms). Braking upgraded to AP Racing 4-piston calipers (front: CP5555-1D, rear: CP5555-2D) with 304mm vented rotors and Ferodo DS2500 pads, complemented by a Wilwood master cylinder (part: 260-11176) for progressive pedal feel.
estetica: Full custom widebody conversion using hand-rolled steel fenders (front: +3.5" per side, rear: +4.0" per side) with integrated vents for brake cooling, finished in a two-tone paint scheme (base: Aston Martin 'Seychelles Blue' with matte black accents). Front bumper replaced with a carbon fiber replica of the Triumph Stag Mk2 prototype (1972 design) with integrated LED turn signals (Lux H8 Angel Eyes) and a custom mesh grille (stainless steel, 3mm wire diameter).

Rear end modified with a custom ducktail spoiler (carbon fiber, 3D-scanned from a 1970s Can-Am race car) and a bespoke exhaust system featuring dual 2.5" stainless steel tips (rolled edges, polished finish) exiting through the rear valance, paired with a custom stainless steel X-pipe (fabricated by Borla, part: 140406). Side skirts replaced with custom aluminum panels (hand-beaten, riveted construction) featuring integrated LED running lights (Philips Ultinon Pro9000).

Interior retrimmed in full Connolly leather (color: 'Chestnut Brown') with contrasting Alcantara headliner and door cards. Seats replaced with Recaro SRD-E (90s WRC-inspired) with custom Triumph Stag embroidery (gold thread) and 4-point harnesses (Schroth Profi II). Dashboard modified with a custom aluminum gauge pod (Veglia Borletti 80mm analog gauges: oil pressure, boost, fuel pressure) and a Momo Prototipo steering wheel (380mm diameter, suede grip) on a NRG quick-release hub.
